title: Cryo-Electron Tomography (cryoET) — in-situ 3D biology at nm scale
sub_domain: In-situ structural biology via cryo tilt-series
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (genesis fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical electron-microscopy literature for this principle.

E.description: Cryo-Electron Tomography (cryoET) — in-situ 3D biology at nm scale: cryo electron transmission produces the measurement through a 5-node primitive DAG L.transmit_sample -> S.scan.angular -> L.backproject -> L.subtomogram_average -> int.angular, with multi-angle tomographic integration and photon-shot-noise-limited (Poisson counting). Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 3D density. Difficulty tier delta=10 with effective condition number kappa_eff~35; calibration-level mismatch (tilt_angle_error, CTF_per_tilt, beam_damage) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: y_theta(r) = integral [rho(r) * CTF_theta(r)] * dose_weight(theta) + n; reconstruct 3D density
E.world_state_x: Scene parameter (density / orientation / spectrum / complex field depending on principle)
E.observation_y: Electron detector output at declared geometry + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D density is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 35); tilt_angle_error dominates the stability cliff; CTF_per_tilt and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Photon-shot-noise-limited (poisson counting) sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while TV / wavelet-sparsity / deep priors stabilise recovery at the ill-conditioned end of Omega.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [WBP] | iterative projection (ADMM / GAP) + optimisation [SIRT] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [IsoNet-cryoET, CryoCARE]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sqrt(sigma_signal^2) / sqrt(photon_count). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |tilt_angle_error| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
